summary:
✅ **Needle found and fix verified!**

I ran `test.sh` and reproduced the issue where the state machine saw duplicate applies after a snapshot and restart. The root cause was that `InstallSnapshot` was updating `lastApplied` but not `commitIndex`, so during a restart the node “forgot” that those snapshot-covered entries were already committed and re-applied them from its log.

### ✅ Fix
In `snapshot.go`, I added logic to bump `commitIndex` to the snapshot’s `LastIncludedIndex` whenever we install a snapshot. That way, the restart path keeps `lastApplied` aligned with the committed snapshot state and doesn’t replay those entries.

### 🔧 Patch (applied)
```go
	// Update lastApplied — the state machine now reflects everything
	// up to and including the snapshot's last index.
	n.lastApplied = snap.LastIncludedIndex

	// The snapshot represents committed state, so ensure commitIndex is
	// also advanced. Without this, a node that restarts soon after
	// installing the snapshot would reset commitIndex to 0 and reapply the
	// snapshot-covered log entries, causing duplicates.
	if n.commitIndex < snap.LastIncludedIndex {
		n.commitIndex = snap.LastIncludedIndex
	}
```

### ✅ Verification
```
./test.sh
=== Raft Snapshot Commit-Gap Test ===
Building...
--- Round 1 ---
PASS: All 13 keys present, no duplicate applies
Round 1: OK
--- Round 2 ---
PASS: All 13 keys present, no duplicate applies
Round 2: OK
--- Round 3 ---
PASS: All 13 keys present, no duplicate applies
Round 3: OK
PASS: No duplicate state machine transitions across all rounds
```

All tests pass across multiple rounds with no duplicate applies—needle safely extracted. 🪡
